excluded_defs.name: get_context
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand MDC API; confirmed get_context() returns thread_local mdc_map_t& which can be copy-initialized directly

exploration_log.aha_moment: mdc::get_context() returns a reference to the thread-local mdc_map_t. Simply copy-constructing mdc_ctx from it in the log_msg_buffer constructor snapshots the originating thread's MDC data before the buffer is handed to the async thread pool.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have added a getter method instead of public member, but public member is simpler and consistent with the struct-like log_msg design.

alternatives: Modify pattern_formatter to accept optional mdc map
Store snapshot in log_msg base struct instead
key_decisions.decision: Add public mdc_map_t mdc_ctx member to log_msg_buffer and snapshot in constructor
key_decisions.reasoning: log_msg_buffer is the right place since it's the owning, serialized form used for cross-thread transfer; log_msg base is intentionally lightweight with string_views only

search_sequence.result: Found buffer only owns logger_name and payload strings; no MDC data

search_sequence.result: Confirmed: log_msg has no MDC field; mdc is thread-local only

solve_notes: Added a public `mdc::mdc_map_t mdc_ctx` member to `log_msg_buffer` and snapshot the current thread's MDC via `mdc::get_context()` in the `log_msg_buffer(const log_msg &)` constructor. Also added `#include <spdlog/mdc.h>` to the header. This makes the originating thread's MDC available to any thread that processes the buffer later (e.g., the async thread pool worker).
task_text:
Fix log_msg_buffer not capturing MDC context for cross-thread consumption

`log_msg_buffer` in `details/log_msg_buffer-inl.h` copies the
`logger_name` and `payload` string_views into an owned buffer, and
copies value-type fields (`level`, `time`, `thread_id`) via the base
`log_msg` copy. However, it does not capture mapped diagnostic context
(MDC) data from `mdc.h`, which is stored in thread-local storage.
When `log_msg_buffer` is consumed on the async logging thread pool
(via `details/thread_pool.h`), the MDC entries from the originating
thread are inaccessible because the worker thread has its own
thread-local storage. Fix `log_msg_buffer` to snapshot the current
thread's MDC data at construction time and make it available when
the buffer is processed on a different thread.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Creates async logger and logs messages, causing log_msg_buffer constructor to be called on the calling thread before the buffer is enqueued to the async thread pool
Tests async logging to file, exercises log_msg_buffer constructor 17000+ times per coverage baseline
Tests multi-thread async logging, exercises log_msg_buffer constructor with concurrent calls from multiple threads

thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms log_msg base struct has no MDC field, so log_msg_buffer is the right place to add the snapshot

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the two edited definitions. The thrash_preventing set adds the mdc class (to get the type name mdc_map_t and the get_context() API) and the log_msg struct (to confirm it has no MDC field and understand what the base copy covers). An AI agent would proactively search for mdc's type before writing mdc::mdc_map_t, and would check log_msg to avoid duplicating any already-captured data.
